title: A changing economy
description: Test your skills on the economic and technological changes of period 9!

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Achievements of the computer revolution thus far extend from the first commercial mainframe, the Univac I introduced in 1954, to the iPhone 6, introduced in September 2014. The rapid development of computing has brought together not just new ideas but ultimately a complete change in how people communicate and obtain information. . . . 

> “The computing revolution has brought a new level of convenience, connectivity, and collaboration to at least 80 percent of the population. A farmer who has a smartphone today has better and faster access to a wide variety of information than a university professor did only two decades ago.”

>-Source: Robert J. Gordon, economist, *The Rise and Fall of American Growth: The U.S. Standard of Living since the Civil War,* 2016

**The excerpt best illustrates which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the advancement in digital communications following several technological innovations
the improvements in educational outcomes as a result of innovative technology like the iPhone
the rapid social and economic changes in the United States after the advent of the computer
the increasing dependence of the American economy on foreign trade as the world became more interconnected

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Achievements of the computer revolution thus far extend from the first commercial mainframe, the Univac I introduced in 1954, to the iPhone 6, introduced in September 2014. The rapid development of computing has brought together not just new ideas but ultimately a complete change in how people communicate and obtain information. . . . 

> “The computing revolution has brought a new level of convenience, connectivity, and collaboration to at least 80 percent of the population. A farmer who has a smartphone today has better and faster access to a wide variety of information than a university professor did only two decades ago.”

>-Source: Robert J. Gordon, economist, *The Rise and Fall of American Growth: The U.S. Standard of Living since the Civil War,* 2016

**Which of the following economic shifts resulted most directly from the trends described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: economic productivity increased
prices of goods decreased
labor became less productive
participation in foreign trade decreased

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Achievements of the computer revolution thus far extend from the first commercial mainframe, the Univac I introduced in 1954, to the iPhone 6, introduced in September 2014. The rapid development of computing has brought together not just new ideas but ultimately a complete change in how people communicate and obtain information. . . . 

> “The computing revolution has brought a new level of convenience, connectivity, and collaboration to at least 80 percent of the population. A farmer who has a smartphone today has better and faster access to a wide variety of information than a university professor did only two decades ago.”

>-Source: Robert J. Gordon, economist, *The Rise and Fall of American Growth: The U.S. Standard of Living since the Civil War,* 2016

**The pattern described in the excerpt most directly contributed to which of the following long-term developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased American participation in the worldwide market with the adoption of new technology
increased consumerism of Americans as the price of new technology decreased
decreased trade between countries as technology widened the gap between producers
decreased productivity for American workers as new technologies became tools for social interaction

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a1bd492e79723ec1031580d9f0f292c0.svg)

**Which of the following was an important direct effect on American society of the developments depicted in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Daily life transformed and new social behaviors emerged.
Standard of living declined for most Americans.
Available jobs in the manufacturing industry decreased.
Educational outcomes for the United States improved.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a1bd492e79723ec1031580d9f0f292c0.svg)

**The overall trend from 1998 to 2010 depicted on the graph led most directly to which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Americans had increased access to information due to technological innovations.
Americans became less economically productive because of new technologies.
Americans’ wages increased to offset the cost of new technologies.
Americans participated less in worldwide trade and e-commerce.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a1bd492e79723ec1031580d9f0f292c0.svg)

**Which of the following statements best explains the change over time in technology adoption depicted in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Technological innovations became entrenched in daily American life.
New technology became too expensive for the average American consumer.
Technological innovations improved the standard of living for Americans.
New technology struggled to gain a foothold as households did not adopt it.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3890848206cfb4fdf51ab407f0f093b8.svg)

> -Source: [Bureau of Labor Statistics](https://www.bls.gov/webapps/legacy/cesbtab1.htm)

**Which of the following factors most directly contributed to the change shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Technological innovations replaced labor in the good-producing and manufacturing industries.
Immigrants replaced Americans in good-producing and manufacturing jobs because they provided cheaper labor.
Stagnation in the 1980s permanently shifted the economy away from relying on manufacturing.
Foreign trade decreased as the United States imposed tariffs to encourage Americans to purchase domestic goods.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/44f42d47ca7ae87c3a536d79ed419297.svg)

**One major change in American labor from 1983 to 2015 was that:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: labor union membership declined.
strikes orchestrated by labor unions increased.
employment in all sectors declined.
good-producing jobs decreased.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3890848206cfb4fdf51ab407f0f093b8.svg)

> -Source: [Bureau of Labor Statistics](https://www.bls.gov/webapps/legacy/cesbtab1.htm)

*Which of the following conclusions is best supported by the data in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Employment in manufacturing and good-producing industries declined, whereas employment in the service sector increased.
Employment in manufacturing industries increased as trade improved, whereas employment in the service sector declined.
Employment in good-producing industries declined, whereas employment in manufacturing industries increased.
Employment in all sectors declined in the years after 2000 as the economy slowed, leading to a recession.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b505ac5db907a59735c191bbc9aef41.svg)

>-Source: [Economic Policy Institute](https://www.epi.org/publication/charting-wage-stagnation/)


**Which of the following statements best explains the overall trend shown in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Wages for very high wage earners increased, whereas wages for middle and low wage earners stagnated.
Wages for all workers steadily increased every year after 1980 as the United States continued to globalize.
Wages increased for middle and working class Americans until an economic recession in 2008.
Wages for top-earning Americans decreased dramatically from 1980 to 2010 as the government addressed the concentration of wealth.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b505ac5db907a59735c191bbc9aef41.svg)

>-Source: [Economic Policy Institute](https://www.epi.org/publication/charting-wage-stagnation/)


**Which of the following was a direct effect of the trend in wages after 1979 shown on the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the concentration of wealth
the improvement in the standard of living
the rise in labor union membership
the start of a period of stagflation

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0b505ac5db907a59735c191bbc9aef41.svg)

>-Source: [Economic Policy Institute](https://www.epi.org/publication/charting-wage-stagnation/)


**The major trend depicted in the graph most directly reflects which of the following developments in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the growing economic inequality between very high wage earners and the working class
the impact of the Great Recession of 2008 on the American economy
the rise in the value of the American dollar as trade increased
the stagnation of wages for earners at all levels as industries struggled
